Change the Uniface Router Startup Parameters

Edit the STARTUP file to change startup parameters.

After installation, the Uniface Router (UROUTER) is started with one command line argument—the TCP port number that was specified during installation. This is compiled into the STARTUP CL program. Therefore, when you wish to change this or add additional command line arguments, you must edit member STARTUP in file STARTUP, recompile it, and restart the Uniface Router.

To do so:

  1. Stop the Uniface Router. For more information, see Start or Stop the Uniface Router on iSeries.

    If you have created a subsystem, stop the subsystem:

    ENDSBS (UNIFxxxx) OPTION(*IMMED)

    If you did not create a subsystem, enter the following command (assuming that UROUTER is listening to port 13001):

    CHGCURLIB UNIFxxxx
CALL PGM(UROUTER) PARM('TCP:+13001' '/shut')
    Note: You can also stop the UROUTER in this way if you have created a subsystem for it, but that will leave the subsystem running. In this case, restarting the UROUTER later on cannot be done by starting the subsystem, but must be done by running STARTUP.
  2. Edit the STARTUP source. For more information, see Installation Contents.

    The following is an example of what your STARTUPCL source could look like:

    PGM

SBMJOB CMD(CALL PGM(UNIFxxxx/UROUTER) +
 PARM('TCP:+13001')) +
 JOB(UROUTER) +
 JOBD(UNIFxxxx/UNIFxxxx) +
 OUTQ(*JOBD) +
 RTGDTA(UNIFACE) +
 CURLIB(UNIFxxxx) +
 CCSID(37) +
 ALWMLTTHD(*YES)
ENDPGM

    Line 4 contains the command line arguments, in this example only PARM('TCP:+13001')When modifying the arguments, ensure the following:

    • Each argument you add is enclosed in single quotes
    • No parentheses or plus signs are removed. [ ) ( + ]
    • Commands in this member are in legal CL syntax
    • Parameters you add are acceptable to the UROUTER. These can be connector assignments and Uniface Router command line switches.

    For example, to change the location of the UROUTER assignment file, you would add the /asn=parameter to line 4:

    PARM('/asn=UNIFxxxx/MYUROUTER.ASN' 'TCP:+13001')) +
  3. Compile the edited STARTUP source, as follows: 
    CRTCLPGM PGM(UNIFxxxx/STARTUP) SRCFILE(UNIFxxxx/STARTUP)

    or with option 14 in PDM (first set the member Type to CLP). If there are no compilation errors, this creates the STARTUPprogram with the new command line arguments for the Uniface Router.

  4. Start the Uniface Router again.

    If you stopped the UROUTER by stopping the subsystem, restart the subsystem again:

    STRSBS UNIFxxxx/UNIFxxxx

    Otherwise, restart it by running STARTUP:

    CALL UNIFxxxx/STARTUP

The Uniface Router has now been started with the new command line arguments. See Ensure that the Uniface Router is Running to confirm that it is really running.

Related Links
  • Command Line Switches Reference

    Command line switches can be used with Uniface executables to specify configuration files, run utilities, supply parameters, apply operating constraints, and otherwise qualify the session. They remain in effect for the duration of that application session.
